volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 1 - Section 220)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Ἥκω νεκρῶν κευθμῶνα καὶ σκότου πύλας
sections.translation: I have come from out of the charnel-house and gates of gloom , where Hades dwells apart from gods , I Polydorus , a son of Hecuba , the daughter of Cisseus , and of Priam . Now my father , when Phrygia ’ s capital

sections.label: Section 5
sections.translation: was threatened with destruction by the spear of Hellas , took alarm and conveyed me secretly from the land of Troy to Polymestor ’ s house , his guest-friend in Thrace , who sows these fruitful plains of Chersonese , curbing by his might a nation delighting in horses .

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: πολὺν δὲ σὺν ἐμοὶ χρυσὸν ἐκπέμπει λάθρᾳ
sections.translation: And with me my father sent much gold by stealth , so that , if ever Ilium ’ s walls should fall , his children that survived might not want for means to live . I was the youngest of Priam ’ s sons ; and this it was that caused my secret removal from the land ; for my childish arm was not able

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: οὔτʼ ἔγχος οἷός τʼ ἦ νέῳ βραχίονι .
sections.translation: to carry weapons or to wield the spear . So long then as the bulwarks of our land stood firm , and Troy ’ s battlements abode unshaken , and my brother Hector prospered in his warring , I , poor child , grew up and flourished , like some vigorous shoot ,

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: τροφαῖσιν ὥς τις πτόρθος ηὐξόμην , τάλας ·
sections.translation: at the court of the Thracian , my father ’ s guest-friend . But when Troy fell and Hector lost his life and my father ’ s hearth was rooted up , and he himself fell butchered at the god-built altar by the hands of Achilles ’ murderous son ;

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: κτείνει με χρυσοῦ τὸν ταλαίπωρον χάριν
sections.translation: then my father ’ s friend killed me , his helpless guest , for the sake of the gold , and then cast me into the swell of the sea , to keep the gold for himself in his house . And there I lie , at one time upon the strand , at another in the salt sea ’ s surge , drifting ever up and down upon the billows ,

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: ἄκλαυτος ἄταφος · νῦν δʼ ὑπὲρ μητρὸς φίλης
sections.translation: unwept , unburied ; but now I am hovering over the head of my dear mother Hecuba , a disembodied spirit , keeping my airy station these three days , ever since my poor mother came from Troy to linger here in the Chersonese .

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: πάντες δʼ Ἀχαιοὶ ναῦς ἔχοντες ἥσυχοι
sections.translation: Meanwhile all the Achaeans sit idly here in their ships at the shores of Thrace ; for the son of Peleus , Achilles , appeared above his tomb and stopped the whole army of Hellas , as they were making straight for home across the sea ,

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: αἰτεῖ δʼ ἀδελφὴν τὴν ἐμὴν Πολυξένην
sections.translation: demanding to have my sister Polyxena offered at his tomb , and to receive his reward . And he will obtain this prize , nor will they that are his friends refuse the gift ; and on this very day fate is leading my sister to her doom .

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: δυοῖν δὲ παίδοιν δύο νεκρὼ κατόψεται
sections.translation: So will my mother see two children dead at once , me and that ill-fated maid . For I , to win a grave , ah me ! will appear among the rippling waves before her servant-maid ’ s feet . Yes ! I have begged this from the powers below ,

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: τύμβου κυρῆσαι κἀς χέρας μητρὸς πεσεῖν .
sections.translation: to find a tomb and fall into my mother ’ s hands . So shall I have my heart ’ s desire ; but now I will get out of the way of aged Hecuba , for here she passes on her way from the shelter of Agamemnon ’ s tent , terrified at my spectre .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ ἥτις ἐκ τυραννικῶν δόμων
sections.translation: Alas ! O mother , from a palace to face a life of slavery , how sad your lot , as sad as once it was blessed ! Some god is now destroying you , setting this in the balance to outweigh your former bliss .

sections.label: Section 59
sections.original: ἄγετʼ , ὦ παῖδες , τὴν γραῦν πρὸ δόμων ,
sections.translation: Guide these aged steps , my servants , forth before the house ;

sections.label: Section 60
sections.translation: guide and support your fellow-slave , once your queen , you maids of Troy . Grasp my aged hand , take me , support me , guide me , lift me up ;

sections.label: Section 65
sections.translation: and I will lean upon your bent arm as on a staff and quicken my halting footsteps onwards . O dazzling light of Zeus ! O gloom of night ! why am I thus scared by

sections.label: Section 70
sections.translation: fearful visions of the night ? O lady Earth , mother of dreams that fly on sable wings ! I am seeking to avert the vision of the night , the sight of horror which I learned from my dreams

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ἀμφὶ Πολυξείνης τε φίλης θυγατρὸς διʼ ὀνείρων
sections.translation: about my son , who is safe in Thrace , and Polyxena , my dear daughter . You gods of this land ! preserve my son ,

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ὃς μόνος οἴκων ἄγκυρʼ ἔτʼ ἐμῶν
sections.translation: the last and only anchor of my house , now settled in Thrace , the land of snow , safe in the keeping of his father ’ s friend . Some fresh disaster is in store , a new strain of sorrow will be added to our woe .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.translation: Such ceaseless thrills of terror never wrung my heart before . Oh ! where , you Trojan maidens , can I find inspired Helenus or Cassandra , that they may read me my dream ?

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: εἶδον γὰρ βαλιὰν ἔλαφον λύκου αἵμονι χαλᾷ
sections.translation: For I saw a dappled deer mangled by a wolf ’ s bloody fangs , torn from my knees by force , piteously . And this too filled me with fear ; over the summit of his tomb appeared Achilles ’ phantom , and for his prize

sections.label: Section 95
sections.translation: he would have one of the luckless maids of Troy . Therefore , I implore you , divine powers , avert this horror from my daughter , from my child .

sections.label: Section 98
sections.translation: Hecuba , I have hastened away to you , leaving my master ’ s tent ,

sections.label: Section 100
sections.translation: where the lot assigned and appointed me as his slave , when I was driven from the city of Ilium , hunted by Achaeans at the point of the spear ; no alleviation do I bring for your sufferings ;

sections.label: Section 105
sections.translation: no , I have laden myself with heavy news , and am a herald of sorrow to you , lady . It is said the Achaeans have determined in full assembly to offer your daughter in sacrifice to Achilles ; for you know how one day he appeared

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: οἶσθʼ ὅτε χρυσέοις ἐφάνη σὺν ὅπλοις ,
sections.translation: standing on his tomb in golden armor , and stayed the sea-borne ships , though they had their sails already hoisted , with this pealing cry : Where away so fast , you Danaids , leaving my tomb

sections.label: Section 115
sections.translation: without its prize ? A violent dispute with stormy altercation arose , and opinion was divided in the warrior army of Hellas , some being in favor of offering the sacrifice at the tomb , others dissenting .

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: ἦν δʼ ὁ τὸ μὲν σὸν σπεύδων ἀγαθὸν
sections.translation: There was Agamemnon , all eagerness in your interest , because of his love for the frenzied prophetess ; but the two sons of Theseus , scions of Athens , though supporting different proposals , yet agreed on the same decision , which was to crown Achilles ’ tomb with fresh blood ;

sections.label: Section 125
sections.translation: for they said they never would set Cassandra ’ s bed before Achilles ’ valor .

sections.label: Section 130
sections.translation: Now the zeal of the rival disputants was almost equal , until that shifty , smooth-mouthed liar , the son of Laertes , whose tongue is always at the service of the mob , persuaded the army not to put aside the best of all the Danaids

sections.label: Section 135
sections.translation: for want of a servant-maid ’ s sacrifice , nor have it said by any of the dead that stand beside Persephone that the Danaids have left the plains of Troy without gratitude

sections.label: Section 140
sections.translation: for their companions who died for Hellas . Odysseus will be here in an instant , to drag the tender maiden from your breast and tear her from your aged arms . Go to the temples , go to the altars ,

sections.label: Section 145
sections.translation: at Agamemnon ’ s knees sit as a suppliant ! Invoke the gods , both those in heaven and those beneath the earth . For either your prayers will avail to spare you the loss of your unhappy child ,

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: ἢ δεῖ σʼ ἐπιδεῖν τύμβου προπετῆ
sections.translation: or you must see your daughter fall before the tomb , her crimson blood spurting in deep dark jets from her neck encircled with gold .

sections.label: Section 154
sections.original: οἲ ἐγὼ μελέα , τί ποτʼ ἀπύσω ;
sections.translation: Woe , woe is me ! What words ,

sections.label: Section 155
sections.translation: or cries , or lamentations can I utter ? Ah me ! for the sorrows of my closing years ! for slavery too cruel to endure , to bear ! Woe , woe is me ! What champion do I have ? Family ,

sections.label: Section 160
sections.translation: and city — where are they ? Aged Priam is no more ; no more my children now . Which way am I to go , either this or that ? Where shall I turn my steps ? Where is any god or divine power to come to my aid ?

sections.label: Section 165
sections.translation: Ah , Trojan maids ! bringers of evil tidings ! Messengers of woe ! you have made an end , an utter end of me ; life on earth has no more charm for me . Oh ! luckless steps , lead on ,

sections.label: Section 170
sections.translation: guide your aged mistress here to the tent . My child , daughter of a most wretched woman , come forth ; listen to your mother ’ s voice .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον ὡς εἰδῇς οἵαν οἵαν
sections.translation: so that you may know the hideous rumor I now hear about your life , my child .

sections.label: Section 177
sections.translation: Oh ! mother , mother , why do you call so loud ? what news is it you have proclaimed , scaring me , like a cowering bird , from my chamber by this alarm ?

sections.label: Section 180
sections.translation: Alas , my daughter !

sections.label: Section 181
sections.original: τί με δυσφημεῖς ; φροίμιά μοι κακά .
sections.translation: Why this ominous address ? it means sorrow for me .

sections.label: Section 182
sections.translation: Woe for your life !

sections.label: Section 183
sections.translation: Tell it , hide it no longer . Ah mother ! how I dread , I dread

sections.label: Section 185
sections.translation: the import of your loud laments .

sections.label: Section 186
sections.translation: Ah my daughter ! a luckless mother ’ s child !

sections.label: Section 187
sections.translation: Why do you tell me this ?

sections.label: Section 188
sections.translation: The Argives with one consent are eager for your sacrifice to the son of Peleus

sections.label: Section 190
sections.translation: at his tomb .

sections.label: Section 191
sections.translation: Ah ! my mother ! how can you speak of such a dire mischance ? Tell me all , mother , yes all !

sections.label: Section 194
sections.translation: It is an ill-boding rumor I tell , my child ;

sections.label: Section 195
sections.translation: they bring me word that sentence is passed upon your life by the Argives ’ vote .

sections.label: Section 197
sections.translation: Alas , for your cruel sufferings , my persecuted mother ! woe for your life of grief ! What grievous outrage

sections.label: Section 200
sections.translation: has some fiend sent on you , hateful , horrible ? No more shall I your daughter share your bondage , hapless youth on hapless age attending .

sections.label: Section 205
sections.translation: For you , alas ! will see me , your hapless child , torn from your arms like a calf of the hills , and sent beneath the darkness of the earth with severed throat for Hades , where with the dead

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: γᾶς ὑποπεμπομέναν σκότον , ἔνθα νεκρῶν μέτα
sections.translation: shall I be laid , ah me ! For your unhappiness I weep with plaintive wail , mother ; but for my own life , its ruin and its outrage , never a tear I shed ; no , death has become to me

sections.label: Section 215
sections.translation: a happier lot than life .

sections.label: Section 216
sections.original: καὶ μὴν Ὀδυσσεὺς ἔρχεται σπουδῇ ποδός ,
sections.translation: See where Odysseus comes in haste , to announce some fresh command to you , Hecuba .

sections.label: Section 218
sections.original: γύναι , δοκῶ μέν σʼ εἰδέναι γνώμην στρατοῦ
sections.translation: Lady , I think you know already the intention of the army , and the vote that has been passed ; still I will declare it .

sections.label: Section 220
sections.translation: It is the Achaeans ’ will to sacrifice your daughter Polyxena at the mound heaped over Achilles ’ grave ; and they appoint me to take the maid and bring her there , while the son of Achilles is chosen to preside over the sacrifice and act as priest .
